title: Calculator Result
description: The canonical shape of a CalculatorResult record — docs/44-PHASE-2B-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §8.2's Calculator Framework design, batch PXP-6 (docs/44 §22), governed by docs/47-PHASE-2B-IMPLEMENTATION-RULES.md, ADR-013 (deterministic, never AI-computed). `input_snapshot` is this schema's second use of docs/44 §11.4's JSON-storage policy (check-in-response.schema.json's `answers` was the first, as that schema's own .md anticipated): stored as a JSON-encoded string in the underlying Sheet cell, but this schema — the entity's real, contractual shape (shared/README.md's 'Contract vs. implementation-only detail') — describes it as a real object, since apps-script/CalculatorResult.gs's own row-to-API-shape conversion always parses it back before returning a record to any caller. Create and list only: no update, no delete, no per-entry get-by-id, the same lifecycle check-in-response.schema.json already establishes. Both calculator_slug and definition_version are stored together, permanently pinning the exact input-field definition that produced this row's input_snapshot even after the calculator is later edited into a newer version (docs/44 §11.4's versioning discipline, applied here identically).

record_id.description: Server-generated UUID (generateFoundationId_()), this row's own identity — never patient-supplied.

patient_id.description: The Patient Identity this result belongs to (docs/33 §1.2, ADR-002). Always session-derived — never client-supplied.

calculator_slug.description: The Calculator this result was computed against, per shared/constants/calculator-registry.json. Paired with definition_version below — pinning both, not just this field, is what keeps this row permanently interpretable even after the calculator is later edited (docs/44 §11.4, applied identically to check-in-response.schema.json's template_id/template_version pairing).

definition_version.description: The exact, immutable Calculator Registry version this result was recorded against (docs/44 §8.2/§11.4). Never re-resolved to a later version after the fact — an existing row is never rewritten to match a newer calculator version.

input_snapshot.description: A flat object of {field_key: value} pairs, one per input field the referenced (calculator_slug, definition_version) declares that the patient actually supplied (docs/44 §11.4: no nested objects or arrays in version 1 of this policy). Keys are written in the calculator's own input_fields-list order at write time, mirroring apps-script/CheckInResponse.gs's foundationBuildDeterministicCheckInAnswers_() precedent, so re-serializing identical inputs never produces a spurious byte-level diff. Stored as a JSON-encoded string in the underlying Sheet cell — an implementation-only detail, not part of this contract (shared/README.md); every function returning a record to a caller parses it back into this real object shape first.

result_value.description: The computed result. Deterministic, doctor/staff-authored formula output only — never AI-computed, adjusted, or interpreted (ADR-013). This generic storage layer never computes or judges this value itself; it is supplied by the caller and stored as-is, validated only for being a flat scalar (never an object/array). Typed as number-or-string rather than number alone, since a future calculator's result might be categorical (e.g. a risk band) rather than numeric.
